title: Replace static ExternalSecrets with operator projection
summary:
Security focus review of CC-0117 (replace static ExternalSecrets with operator projection). No security issues found. OpenBao policy changes are comment-only verification notes in the established CC-0112/CC-0116 style — git diff over deploy/openbao/policies/*.hcl shows no grant/capability changes and no path widening; the operator-projected admin-password ExternalSecret reads bootstrap/{ns}/{keystone}/admin which is already covered by the existing read-only kv-v2/data/bootstrap/* grant. setup-auth.sh role bindings untouched. No hardcoded secrets (the //nolint:gosec G101 on adminPasswordSecretNameSuffix is a correct false-positive suppression — it is a Secret NAME suffix, not a credential). The admin-password Secret is ESO-owned (CreationPolicy: Owner) and watched via an index-backed MapFunc, not EnqueueRequestForOwner, so the credential-rotation watch is functional.

Quality focus review of CC-0117. reconcile_adminpassword.go is a faithful 1:1 mirror of the merged reconcile_dbcredentials.go (CC-0116) — same builder/helper/sub-reconciler shape, esov1 API, ClusterSecretStore ref, CreatePolicyOwner, CreateOrUpdate+SetControllerReference, WaitForExternalSecret gate, dedicated requeue constant, ObservedGeneration on every condition. effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef is used at all three readers (reconcile_keystone.go:138, reconcile_korc.go:890, controlplane_controller.go:218). golangci-lint reports 0 issues, go vet clean, gofumpt clean. Two MINOR comment-staleness nits remain (the helper's NOTE and the test-file package comment both still describe the consumers as wired in a 'later level' / 'NOT exercised here', which is now obsolete since all consumers are wired). These are suggestions only and do not affect the verdict.

Testing focus review of CC-0117. Test coverage is comprehensive across all existing tiers (unit, envtest integration, Chainsaw e2e). reconcile_adminpassword_test.go mirrors reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go with all five required tests (Managed_CreatesExternalSecret, NotReady_SetsConditionFalseAndRequeues, Ready_SetsConditionTrue, Brownfield_NoExternalSecret_ReadyTrue, RemoteKeyFor_And_SecretName_DistinctPerControlPlane). The corrected TestReconcileKeystone_ClusterRefsDerivedFromInfrastructure assertion, the new managed-override/brownfield Keystone tests, the readAdminPassword managed test, the extractor managed/brownfield tests, and the new integration phase + TestIntegration_AdminPasswordGate_BlocksKeystoneUntilExternalSecretReady are all present. All unit tests pass (go test -count=1) and all 6 envtest integration tests pass (285s, incl. the new gate test). Assertions are specific (owner-ref kind/name, remoteRef key+property, CreationPolicy, distinct-per-CP table, non-mutation of cp.Spec).

tests_checklist.item: Secret-handling paths covered by tests (managed reads operator-owned Secret; brownfield ref preserved)
Behavior covered by tests; comment-only nits have no behavioral impact
Tests exist and pass (unit + integration, uncached)
Edge cases covered: managed, brownfield, ES-not-ready, ES-ready, distinct-per-CP (ns/name), legacy keystone-admin non-collision
Error/gate cases tested: AdminPassword gate blocks Keystone projection until ES Ready (integration)
Assertions specific (exact owner-ref, remoteRef key/property, condition reason, requeue interval)
Test tier completeness: unit + envtest integration + Chainsaw e2e all extended
Non-mutation asserted: cp.Spec.KORC.AdminCredential.PasswordSecretRef untouched in brownfield + managed override

code_quality_checklist.item: No secrets passed as command-line args / no in-process secret exposure introduced
Q3 DRY: mirrors CC-0116 structure, reuses childNamespace/keystoneName/openBaoClusterStoreName/secrets.WaitForExternalSecret
Q4 naming: adminPassword* names parallel dbCredential* names consistently
Q6 no magic strings: condition type via conditionTypeAdminPasswordReady constant; reason strings inline exactly as the DB sibling does
PC1/PC3: same concept implemented the same way across DB-credential and admin-password reconcilers
D1/D3: two stale 'later level' comments remain (helper + test package doc) — minor
New tests mirror the CC-0116 DB-credential test shape (no novel ad-hoc structure)
Fixture spec ref kept != projected name so override assertions genuinely prove the override (documented DECISION)

security_checklist.item: S3: No hardcoded secrets — adminPasswordSecretNameSuffix is a name suffix with correct //nolint:gosec G101 annotation
OpenBao policy diffs are comment-only, read-only, no path widening (eso-management.hcl, eso-control-plane.hcl)
ESO ExternalSecret reads bootstrap/{ns}/{keystone}/admin under the pre-existing kv-v2/data/bootstrap/* read grant — no new write/wildcard scope
S5: admin password never logged; condition messages carry no secret material
Watch on ESO-owned Secret uses index-backed MapFunc (not EnqueueRequestForOwner) so rotation events are not silently dropped
//nolint:gosec G101 annotations correct and consistent with dbCredentialSecretNameSuffix
e2e admin-password Secret diagnostics dump keys only (describe), not values

architecture_checklist.item: Per-CP Secret name + per-CP OpenBao path prevent cross-ControlPlane credential collision
Surgical: webhook untouched, keystone operator untouched, ControlPlaneSecretNameIndexKey string constant unchanged (only doc comments)
Per-CP unique Secret name + OpenBao path (avoids the hardcoded-sub-resource-name multi-instance failure)
Drift guard TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames passes with the new AdminPassword entry

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No security-mitigation duplication or divergence vs sibling reconciler
No speculative abstraction; effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef is a single 5-line helper consumed by 3 call sites
Integration sync helper factored (simulateAdminPasswordExternalSecretSyncWhenPresent) mirroring the DB analog

fail_fast_checklist.item: ExternalSecret errors surface as AdminPasswordReady=False with explicit reason, not swallowed
ExternalSecret create/check errors set AdminPasswordReady=False and return the error
Chainsaw admin-password-scoping uses set -euo pipefail with assert_eq fail-and-exit; presence guard exits 0 only on absent stack

defensive_checklist.item: effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef returns SecretRefSpec by value (plain string struct) — cannot alias/mutate cp.Spec
SecretRefSpec returned by value (plain string struct) — no cp.Spec aliasing
e2e presence guard (CRDs + ClusterSecretStore) prevents hard-fail on stacks without the operator

issues_found.description: Stale comment and now-obsolete //nolint:unused directive on effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef. The NOTE states 'callers of effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef are added in Level 2 — defining it now is intentional; it is not wired into any consumer yet.' and the //nolint:unused directive both describe a state that no longer holds: the helper is wired into all three consumers (reconcile_keystone.go:138, reconcile_korc.go:890, controlplane_controller.go:218). golangci-lint passes either way (nolintlint is disabled), so this is cosmetic, but the comment is now factually inaccurate (review pattern: check-comments-describe-the-actual-invariant).
issues_found.fix: Remove the two-line NOTE paragraph (lines 83-85) and the //nolint:unused directive (line 86), since the function is now referenced by three production call sites.

issues_found.description: Stale 'later level' phrasing in the reconcile_adminpassword_test.go package comment. It states the projected secretRef override '(REQ-005, effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef) is wired into consumers in a later level and is NOT exercised here.' The 'wired into consumers in a later level' phrasing reads as not-yet-done; the override is now fully wired and exercised by reconcile_keystone_test.go / reconcile_korc_test.go / setupwithmanager_test.go / integration_test.go. The 'NOT exercised here' part remains accurate for this file, but the forward-looking phrasing is obsolete.
issues_found.fix: Reword to state that the override is exercised in the Keystone/KORC/extractor/integration test files, and this file scopes only the ExternalSecret projection.

suggested_improvements: Optionally align reconcile_keystone.go's admin-password override to the same explicit `if ClusterRef != nil` guard shape used for the DB-credential override (lines 123-125) for visual symmetry; the current unconditional `= effectiveAdminPasswordSecretRef(cp)` is behaviorally identical (brownfield returns the spec ref verbatim) and arguably cleaner, so this is purely stylistic.

TestAdminPasswordRemoteKeyFor_And_SecretName_DistinctPerControlPlane and its DB-credential sibling are near-identical in shape; a future table-driven consolidation across credential families could reduce duplication (non-blocking, matches the consolidate-near-duplicate-tests advisory).
